claims.text: The AI Scientist enables frontier large language models to perform research independently and communicate their findings through a comprehensive framework for fully automated scientific discovery.
The AI Scientist-v2 demonstrates that an autonomous AI system can produce peer-reviewed workshop papers, marking the first instance of a fully AI-generated paper successfully navigating peer review.
Agent Laboratory is an autonomous LLM-based framework that can complete the entire research process from idea to results while reducing costs by 84%.
MLE-bench provides a standardized benchmark for assessing the capabilities of ML agents in performing ML engineering tasks
AIBuildAI-2, a knowledge-enhanced agent with an evolving hierarchical knowledge system, can autonomously build AI models at a level comparable to expert human practitioners.

Reflexion demonstrates that language agents can be effectively reinforced through verbal feedback and reflection rather than weight updates, achieving significant performance improvements across diverse tasks.
SELF-REFINE can significantly improve LLM outputs through iterative self-feedback without requiring additional training, data, or reinforcement learning.
VOYAGER is the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that can continuously explore the world, acquire diverse skills, and make novel discoveries without human intervention.

Top commercial LLMs demonstrate strong agent capabilities while open-source models show significant performance gaps, with poor long-term reasoning, decision-making, and instruction following identified as main obstacles.
LM agents require specially-built interfaces to effectively perform software engineering tasks, similar to how humans need IDEs.
This paper proposes a learning method for Radio Frequency Fingerprint (RFF) identification in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) to address authentication challenges.
Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks have become research focal points due to their effectiveness in modeling non-linear, time-variant hydrological systems.
This paper introduces a novel Transformer attention mechanism inspired by the Kolmogorov-Arnold representation theorem.

Expression-based ML models can serve as cost-efficient screening tools in resource-limited contexts, while personalized gene regulatory networks analyzed with graph neural networks can capture patient-specific regulatory patterns for metastasis prediction.
The proposed Multisize Patched Spatial-Temporal Transformer Network effectively addresses the challenges of both short- and long-term urban crowd flow prediction.
